steps.title: Find the clue about shared traits
steps.content: The passage lists protective spines, pointed snouts, and small body size as features the tenrecs share with true hedgehogs, indicating a relationship of similarity despite distant relation.

steps.title: Choose the word that means 'shared features'
steps.content:
The phrase needs to complete "share basic ___ true hedgehogs" in a way that describes shared traits like spines and pointed snouts. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — examples of: "share basic examples of true hedgehogs" — grammatically awkward and implies tenrecs are instances of hedgehogs, not related by shared features. ✗
  • Choice B — concerns about: "share basic concerns about true hedgehogs" — implies they worry about hedgehogs, which makes no sense in context. ✗
  • Choice C — indications of: "share basic indications of true hedgehogs" — suggests the tenrecs are signs or evidence of hedgehogs, not that they share physical traits. ✗
  • Choice D — similarities with: "share basic similarities with true hedgehogs" — correctly expresses that both groups independently developed the same traits. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
